_meta.keyKnowledge: We investigate how media artists, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples, celebrate and challenge multiple perspectives of Australian identity.

_source.w: We investigate how media artists, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples, celebrate and challenge multiple perspectives of Australian identity.
y: Australian media arts is enriched by diverse perspectives that celebrate and question what it means to be Australian.
First Nations media artists are leading voices in redefining Australian identity and challenging dominant narratives.
Investigating these works develops our understanding of representation, power and voice in media.
eg: We study media works by Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander artists that celebrate cultural identity.
We analyse how First Nations media artists challenge stereotypes and dominant narratives.
We investigate how diverse Australian media artists represent and question Australian identity.
We discuss issues of representation, ownership and self-determination in Australian media.
We explore how media arts support cultural revitalisation and community empowerment.
We engage critically with questions about whose stories are told, by whom and for whom.
